title: Petrobras Reports on Investments in Refining and Petrochemicals in Rio de Janeiro
content:
Petrobras is planning investments of around R$33 billion in refining and petrochemical projects in Rio de Janeiro, with R$29 billion from Petrobras itself and R$4 billion from a synergistic project. A significant portionГўв‚¬вЂќR$26 billionГўв‚¬вЂќwill fund the integration of the Boaventura Energy Complex (ItaboraГѓВ­) with Reduc (Duque de Caxias), aiming to boost fuel production and infrastructure modernization.

The Boaventura Complex will notably increase the output of S-10 diesel by 76,000 bpd, aviation kerosene (QAV) by 20,000 bpd, and Group II lubricants by 12,000 bpd. It will also house a dedicated BioQAV plant capable of producing 19,000 bpd of renewable fuels (HVO and SAF), alongside two gas-fired thermoelectric plants, which will leverage existing infrastructure from the Natural Gas Processing Unit in ItaboraГѓВ­.

At Reduc, a 30,000 mГ‚Ві/month lubricant re-refining project is under study, aligned with circular economy principles. Co-processing tests for this project have already been authorized by ANP. Reduc has also successfully tested SAF production from corn oil and plans to begin commercial production soon at 10,000 bpd capacity. In addition, it produces Diesel R5 and is preparing to test Diesel R7, demonstrating PetrobrasГўв‚¬в„ў push toward product decarbonization.

Modernization at Reduc will include a new thermoelectric plant, replacing outdated systems, with R$860 million in investment. A further R$2.4 billion is earmarked for maintenance shutdowns between 2025Гўв‚¬вЂњ2029, with significant work planned for 2026.

On the petrochemical front, Petrobras is exploring the domestic production of acetic acid and monoethylene glycol (MEG) at Boaventura to reduce reliance on imports. Additionally, BraskemГўв‚¬вЂќa Petrobras subsidiaryГўв‚¬вЂќplans to expand its polyethylene plant using Route 3 gas, potentially adding 230,000 tons/year in capacity with a R$4 billion investment, pending governance approvals.
author: Analysts Account
